description_long: This house features a two-story facade with a weathered, stucco exterior and exposed brickwork at the lower level, giving it a rustic, Mediterranean appearance. The front is adorned with three tall, arched windows on the upper floor, each with white trim and wooden shutters, while the ground floor has a large double-door entry flanked by two smaller windows with open shutters. The roof is covered with reddish-orange terracotta tiles and includes a small dormer and a brick chimney. The side walls show signs of aging, with patches of exposed brick and worn plaster, adding to the building's character. The rear of the house is plain, with no windows or doors, and displays some weathering and minor surface damage. Overall, the structure suggests a traditional, urban residential building with historical charm.
description: A two-story, Mediterranean-style house with a terracotta-tiled roof, arched windows, and weathered stucco and brick exterior.
description_view_0: Front view showing three arched upper windows with shutters, two lower windows with open shutters, and a large double-door entry.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying weathered stucco, exposed brick patches, and no windows or doors.
description_view_2: Rear view with a plain, weathered stucco wall, no windows or doors, and minor surface damage.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with visible exposed brick and worn plaster, no windows or doors.
attribute_entryway_design: Large double wooden doors with a decorative frame, flanked by windows with open shutters, no porch or steps.
